title: Aloo Dum

ingredients.title: For the Potatoes
items: 500 g baby potatoes or medium potatoes, peeled and halved

ingredients.title: For the Gravy
items: 1 tsp amchur (dry mango powder) or 1 tsp lemon juice

ingredients.title: To Garnish
items: Optional: a few drops of kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves)

instruction.title: 1. Boil and Prepare Potatoes
steps: Boil potatoes in salted water with ¼ tsp turmeric until just tender (not falling apart). Drain and set aside.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan and lightly sauté the boiled potatoes until they get a golden crust. Remove and keep aside.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare the Gravy
steps: In the same pan, heat 2 tbsp oil or ghee. Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamoms, and cloves. Sauté for 30 seconds until aromatic.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1-2 minutes until raw aroma disappears.

instruction.title: 3. Spice and Simmer
steps: Add tomato puree, turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until oil separates from the masala.
Pour in 1 cup water or stock and bring to a gentle simmer.

instruction.title: 4. Combine Potatoes and Gravy
steps: Add the sautéed potatoes to the gravy. Mix gently to coat the potatoes with the masala.
Sprinkle garam masala and amchur powder (or lemon juice). Cover and simmer on low heat for 8–10 minutes, allowing the potatoes to absorb the flavors.

instruction.title: 5. Finishing Touch
steps: Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and optional kasuri methi.
Serve hot with steamed rice, naan, or roti.

chefTips: For extra depth, roast the spices (cumin, cardamom, cinnamon) lightly before adding onions.
Using baby potatoes gives a creamier texture; regular potatoes work fine too.
Adjust chili powder to taste; smoky paprika can give a milder, aromatic heat.
